title: Article Auto-Linking Worker

story.i_want_to: automatically discover and create relationships between knowledge articles based on semantic similarity
story.so_that: the knowledge graph builds itself as new articles are added, without manual curation

rationale: A knowledge wiki without links is just a list. Manually curating relationships between articles is tedious and scales poorly as the wiki grows. By comparing article embeddings when new articles are stored, the system can automatically discover semantically related articles and create `relates_to` links. Optionally, it can detect contradictions by calling the ExtractorBehaviour. This transforms the flat article collection into a connected knowledge graph that surfaces related context automatically.

acceptance_criteria.description: A `Loopctl.Workers.ArticleLinkingWorker` Oban worker exists in the `:knowledge` queue with max_attempts: 3. It receives article_id and tenant_id in the job args.
The worker is triggered after an embedding is stored for a new article. It is enqueued from the embedding storage callback or pipeline (following the pattern established in US-20.3/US-20.4).

The worker fetches the new article's embedding and compares it via cosine similarity against all other article embeddings in the same tenant. If project_id is set on the article, comparison is scoped to the same project; if project_id is nil (tenant-wide), comparison runs against all tenant articles.

A configurable similarity threshold (default 0.8) is read from Application config: `Application.get_env(:loopctl, :article_link_threshold, 0.8)`. Articles exceeding the threshold get `relates_to` links created.
Created ArticleLinks have link_type: :relates_to and metadata: %{auto_generated: true, similarity_score: float}. The similarity score is stored for transparency.
No duplicate links: before creating a link, the worker checks if a link already exists between the two articles (in either direction). If so, it skips creation.

Optionally, the worker calls `ExtractorBehaviour.detect_contradiction/2` (if the callback is defined) to check if high-similarity articles actually contradict each other. If so, the link is created with link_type: :contradicts instead of :relates_to.
Rate limiting: the worker processes at most 50 similarity comparisons per execution. If there are more candidate articles, it logs a warning and processes the top 50 by recency.
The worker is idempotent: re-running for the same article_id does not create duplicate links. Existing links are detected and skipped.
An audit event is created on completion: event_type "knowledge.articles_linked", payload includes article_id and count of new links created.

The worker resolves the embedding provider via compile-time DI: @embedding_client Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingClient). The same config key and behaviour from US-20.3 is reused.

The worker resolves the extractor for contradiction detection via compile-time DI: @extractor Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :knowledge_extractor, Loopctl.Knowledge.LlmExtractor). The same config key and behaviour from US-21.1 is reused.
The worker implements a custom backoff: @impl Oban.Worker def backoff(%Oban.Job{attempt: attempt}), do: trunc(:math.pow(attempt, 4) + 15 + :rand.uniform(30) * attempt).
The worker uses Oban unique option: unique: [period: 300, keys: [:article_id]] to prevent duplicate linking jobs for the same article.
The maximum number of comparison articles is configurable via Application.get_env(:loopctl, :article_link_max_comparisons, 50).

test_cases.name: Worker creates relates_to links for similar articles
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as existing_article with embedding stored
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as new_article with embedding stored
Cosine similarity between embeddings exceeds 0.8 threshold
expected_results: Link metadata contains auto_generated: true and similarity_score as float

test_cases.name: Worker skips articles below similarity threshold
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as existing_article with dissimilar embedding
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as new_article with embedding stored
Cosine similarity between embeddings is below 0.8

test_cases.name: Worker does not create duplicate links on re-run
preconditions: Two articles with high similarity and an existing relates_to link between them

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: worker does not link across tenants
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id}) as article_a with embedding
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_b.id}) as article_b with similar embedding
expected_results: No links created to tenant_b's articles
Similarity comparison only includes tenant_a articles

test_cases.name: Worker respects rate limit of 50 comparisons
preconditions: 60 articles exist with embeddings for the tenant
steps: Execute ArticleLinkingWorker.perform/1 for a new article
expected_results: Only top 50 articles by recency are compared
Warning logged about exceeding comparison limit

test_cases.name: Worker handles article with no embedding gracefully
preconditions: fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as article with no embedding stored
expected_results: Worker returns {:error, :no_embedding} or :ok with no links created

technical_notes: Cosine similarity can be computed in PostgreSQL using pgvector's `<=>` operator or in Elixir. Prefer the database query for efficiency: `SELECT id, 1 - (embedding <=> $1) as similarity FROM article_embeddings WHERE tenant_id = $2 AND article_id != $3 ORDER BY similarity DESC LIMIT 50`.
The similarity threshold should be configurable via Application config, NOT hardcoded. Default 0.8 is a reasonable starting point but may need tuning per deployment.
Duplicate link detection must check both directions: article_a -> article_b AND article_b -> article_a, since ArticleLinks are directional but relates_to is semantically bidirectional.

The contradiction detection callback is optional -- if ExtractorBehaviour does not define detect_contradiction/2, the worker should skip that step. Use `function_exported?/3` or a separate optional callback behaviour.
Testing: async: true, Mox for EmbeddingBehaviour and ExtractorBehaviour, fixture(:article) with embeddings, tenant isolation test, setup :verify_on_exit!.
Rate limiting at 50 comparisons prevents runaway compute on tenants with large article collections. The limit should be configurable via Application config.
